tool.name: Flexynesis Inference
tool.description: Run inference using a pretrained Flexynesis model
tool.command:
@CHECK_NON_COMMERCIAL_USE@ export TORCHINDUCTOR_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/torch_cache && mkdir -p input/test output &&
ln -s '$test_clin' input/test/clin.tabular && python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' input/test/clin.tabular input/test/clin.csv &&
#if str($assay_main) != '': #set $name = str($assay_main.replace(" ", "_")) ln -s '$test_omics_main' input/test/${name}.tabular && python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' input/test/${name}.tabular input/test/${name}.csv &&
#set $data_names = [$name] #else ln -s '$test_omics_main' input/test/main.tabular && python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' input/test/main.tabular input/test/main.csv && #set $data_names = ['main'] #end if #for $i, $element in enumerate($omics) #if str($element.test_omics) != 'None': #if str($element.assay) != '': #set $i = str($element.assay.replace(" ", "_")) ln -s '${element.test_omics}' input/test/${i}.tabular && python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' input/test/${i}.tabular input/test/${i}.csv &&
$data_names.append(str($i)) #else ln -s '${element.test_omics}' input/test/omics_${i}.tabular && python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' input/test/omics_${i}.tabular input/test/omics_${i}.csv &&
$data_names.append("omics_" + str($i)) #end if #end if #end for python3 '$__tool_directory__/convert_joblib_json.py' --convert json_joblib -i '$artifacts' -o input/artifacts.joblib && python3 '$__tool_directory__/convert_safetensors_pth.py' --safetensors "$model" --config "$config" --artifacts "$artifacts" --output "input/model.pth" &&
#if $target_variables == "True": ## Extract target variables from config.json target_vars=\$(python -c "import json; config=json.load(open('$config')); tv=config.get('target_variables'); print(','.join(tv) if tv else 'None')") && echo "Target variables from config: \$target_vars" && #end if
#if $survival == "True": ## Extract survival variables from config.json surv_event=\$(python -c "import json; config=json.load(open('$config')); print(config.get('surv_event_var') or 'None')") && surv_time=\$(python -c "import json; config=json.load(open('$config')); print(config.get('surv_time_var') or 'None')") && echo "Survival event variable from config: \$surv_event" && echo "Survival time variable from config: \$surv_time" && #end if
flexynesis --pretrained_model input/model.pth --artifacts input/artifacts.joblib --data_path_test input/test --outdir output --prefix infered --target_variables "\$target_vars" --surv_event_var "\$surv_event" --surv_time_var "\$surv_time" --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4}" \${GALAXY_FLEXYNESIS_EXTRA_ARGUMENTS} &&
## convert flexynesis output files to tabular format for file in output/*.csv; do if [ -f "\$file" ]; then basename="\${file%.csv}"; python '$__tool_directory__/convert_tabular_csv.py' "\$file" "\${basename}.tabular"; echo "Converted \$file to \${basename}.tabular"; fi done

param.name: target_variables
param.label: Use target variable for prediction
param.help: Set this to True, if the model was trained with target_variable

param.name: survival
param.label: Use survival variable for prediction
param.help: Set this to True, if the model was trained with survival data

tool.help:
@COMMON_HELP@
**Flexynesis Inference Mode**
This tool runs inference using a pretrained Flexynesis model on new test data, without retraining.
-----
**When to use inference mode**
Use this tool when you have:
1. A trained model from a previous Flexynesis training run 2. The corresponding artifacts file from that training run 3. The model configuration file from that training run 4. New samples you want to classify/predict using the trained model
-----
**Automatic parameter extraction**
The tool automatically extracts the following parameters from the model configuration file:
- **Target variables**: Variable(s) the model was trained to predict - **Survival variables**: Event and time variables if the model was trained for survival analysis
This ensures that inference runs with the same settings as training, generating both embeddings and predictions.
-----
.. _Documentation: https://bimsbstatic.mdc-berlin.de/akalin/buyar/flexynesis/site/ .. _copyright holders: https://github.com/BIMSBbioinfo/flexynesis
